title: Gnaphalium recurvatum
taxonomy: Gnaphalium recurvatum is a species of flowering plant that is classified under the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it within the order Asterales and the clade angiosperms, which includes flowering plants. Gnaphalium recurvatum is further categorized within the genus Gnaphalium, a group of plants that are often referred to as cudweeds. The plant is native to the western United States and is commonly found in areas with dry to moist soils. Its classification is based on its shared characteristics and genetic relationships with other species within the Asteraceae family.
uses: Gnaphalium recurvatum has been traditionally used by Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties.
